preamble: The shift from the voiced alveolar fricative [z] to the voiceless velar fricative [x] is a documented multi-stage process involving devoicing followed by retraction, notably observed in the historical development of Spanish sibilants.

examples.note: Major sibilant shift in the 16th century where voiced alveolar fricatives devoiced to [s] and subsequently retracted to the velar fricative [x] (e.g., in the development of 'jota').

sources: Penny, R. (2002). A History of the Spanish Language.
